matches.rationale: Same Edit target: edits below the repository root require reading README files along the target path. The extraction narrows the covered top-level directories, which is partial coverage.
Same Write target: creation below the repository root requires reading README files along the target path. The extraction narrows the covered top-level directories, which is partial coverage.

Same Edit target and prerequisite: start the filtered Next.js dev watch before changing Next.js source or tests. The extracted test path is broader than integration tests and omits the explicit explanation-on-skip clause.
The Bash rule's evidence specifically directs using the fast Next.js types command instead of a build for type errors; its command pattern only detects the disfavored build side.
Same Bash target: a git checkout/branch switch requires the full bootstrap build.
Same Write target: creating a test file must go through pnpm new-test rather than manual creation. The pattern covers conventional test/spec filenames only.
The evidence targets the same prohibited rerunning of a test suite for different grep analyses and directs capturing output once. The regex is broader and also covers builds.
Same Edit target: setTimeout-based waiting in tests is prohibited. The extraction covers only the forbidden half and omits the required retry() replacement.
Same Edit target: adding deprecated check() in TypeScript tests is blocked in favor of retry() plus expect().
Same Edit target: an inline files object in test fixture configuration should be replaced by a real fixture directory.
Same Bash target: mode-specific local test commands must reproduce the CI environment flags. The extraction states this specifically for snapshot updates and omits the IS_WEBPACK_TEST example.
Same Bash target: gh pr create must include the required HTML marker at the bottom of the PR description. Updating an existing description is not covered.
Same Bash target: commands must not expose secret values in emitted logs or commits. The regex detects a limited set of literal secret forms and the evidence also mentions chat output.
Same Bash target: staging local environment/key files for a commit is prohibited. The extraction detects git add and omits the placeholder-only documentation clause.
Same Bash target: mutating or adding a remote as an authentication workaround is prohibited unless requested. The regex does not encode the preceding SSH-failure condition.
Same Bash target: force pushes and rebases that may hydrate partial-clone objects should be preceded by an SSH preflight. The pattern cannot restrict itself to partial clones.
Same Bash target: git commit messages must not contain Generated with Claude Code or Co-Authored-By footers.
Same Bash target: committing after changes requires pre-validation to avoid lint-staged failures. The extraction omits the specific formatting and lint checks.
Same Bash target: gh pr ready is blocked so the PR remains draft for the user.
Same Bash target: running a mode-specific Next.js integration test after source changes requires rebuilding first.
Same Edit target: a new flag must be wired through its type, schema, and define-env when user-bundled. The extraction also names additional possible files.
Same Edit target: react-server-dom-webpack imports outside entry-base.ts are prohibited and consumers should use exported component modules.
Same Edit target: relative internal require calls in app-page.ts are prohibited because they cannot resolve from a user project.
Same Edit target: browser.back() must not return to a router-act page where accordion links are already visible. The path pattern is broader than router-act tests.
Same Edit target: new Rust super:: imports are discouraged except inside inline mod blocks, with crate-rooted paths preferred.
Same Edit target: adding highlight={...} in Markdown requires counting actual code-block lines. The extraction omits the clause to highlight only relevant code.
Same Edit target: new nonstandard request-header reads in Next.js server code must be flagged for security review.

misses.note: No Write rule requires starting watch mode before creating Next.js source or integration-test files; the analogous extracted rule is Edit-only.
No extracted Bash rule represents keeping the already-started watch process alive throughout iteration.
No extracted Bash rule selects the focused test script from both mode and bundler context.
Compiler skip: "Kill the background watch process when you're done iterating (no specific triggering tool call)".
No extracted rule targets the filtered Next.js build specifically after bootstrap when changes are limited to core files.
No extracted rule captures the compound contexts requiring build-all: bootstrap/branch switch, pre-CI push, or cross-package changes.
No extracted Bash rule requires --webpack when selecting Webpack for next build or next dev.
Compiler skip: "There is no `--no-turbopack` flag — factual note, not an instruction".
The compiler produced only a Write rule for test-file creation, not a Bash rule governing invocation of the mandatory pnpm new-test generator.
No extracted Bash rule captures AI-agent non-interactive pnpm new-test invocation with forwarded --args and the documented argument format.
Compiler skip: "Run `scripts/pr-status.js` when the user asks about CI/PR status — the trigger is a user question, not a constraint on an outgoing tool call".

Compiler skip: "General PR-status triage prioritization (blocking failures first; assume failures real until disproven; use Known Flaky Tests as context not auto-dismissal) — process/judgment guidance".

Compiler skip: "General PR-status triage prioritization (blocking failures first; assume failures real until disproven; use Known Flaky Tests as context not auto-dismissal) — process/judgment guidance".
Compiler skip: "Use the normal mode-specific test command for module-resolution/build-graph fixes — the triggering condition isn't detectable from command syntax alone".
No Bash extraction binds automated PR/CI analysis to the referenced pr-status-triage skill.
No extracted Bash rule represents the judgment to prioritize CI failures over review comments.
No extracted rule maps a rust check/build CI failure to cargo fmt -- --check followed by cargo fmt.
No extracted rule maps a lint/build Prettier failure to pnpm prettier --write on the affected file.
No extracted rule requires running the specific failing test path for a CI test failure.
No remaining extracted rule selects the development or production test script matching a failing mode.
Compiler skip: "Development Tips env-var suggestions (DEBUG=next:*, NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1) — documentation catalog, no obligation attached".
Compiler skip: "Development Tips env-var suggestions (DEBUG=next:*, NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1) — documentation catalog, no obligation attached".
Compiler skip: "NODE_ENV vs __NEXT_DEV_SERVER explanation — explanatory/documentation of semantics, not a prescriptive action rule".
Compiler skip: "NODE_ENV vs __NEXT_DEV_SERVER explanation — explanatory/documentation of semantics, not a prescriptive action rule".
No extracted Bash rule covers treating all environment-variable values as sensitive except known test flags; the secret-literal extraction is narrower and is assigned to g38.
No remaining extracted rule combines exact CI names/modes with the prohibition on literal secrets; the CI-env extraction is assigned to the closer reproduction rule g24.
Compiler skip: "If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials — behavioral judgment, not a specific tool call".
Compiler skip: "Keep commit messages concise and descriptive — tone/style".
Compiler skip: "PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why — tone/style".
No extracted Edit rule forces edge-build flags guarding Node-only imports to false in define-env.ts; the generic compile-time require rule has a different trigger.

unmatched_extracted.note: The document contains a real Bash-bindable rule to save external API output and avoid fetching the same data repeatedly, but no gold rule lists it.
The document genuinely prohibits AI-generation/co-author footers in PR descriptions as well as commits, while gold g44 covers commits only.
The document contains a real Edit-bindable DCE rule requiring require() calls to remain behind compile-time if/else branches; no gold rule lists that target.
